title: Grimoire
description: Spellcasting maximums derived from CORE + DB. Carries only maximum values — no remaining counters. Deterministic: the deriver computes everything from CORE choices; no player decisions beyond what's already in CORE. Content-neutral: catalog-driven.

derived_from_core.description: Hash of the CORE sheet this grimoire was derived from. Mismatch triggers re-derivation.

sources.description: Every source granting this character spells, keyed by source identifier. A class, feat, species, or item.

spells.description: The character's spells with full DB metadata. Unique by (name, source).

spell_slots.description: Leveled spell slot maximums by spell level. Keys are spell levels as strings '1'..'9'. NO remaining counters.

pact_slots.description: Pact Magic slot maximums by spell level. NO remaining counters.

castingSource.description: One source of spells with its own spellcasting ability and budgets. Kind + ability + cantrips_known + prepared_limit. No derived modifier/save_dc/attack_bonus — those live in MODIFIER.

kind.description: What the source is: a class, subclass, feat, species, lineage, or item.

cantrips_known.description: Cantrips this source grants/knows at the current level.

prepared_limit.description: Level-1+ spells this source can prepare (the budget). null for non-preparing casters (e.g. pact-magic casters, feat-granted spells).

ability_mode.description: How the spellcasting ability was resolved. 'choose' (e.g. species-lineage grants with per-player ability choice), 'increased' (feat-granted spells keyed to the feat's ability increase), or null for fixed-ability sources.

spell.description: One spell the character holds. Three independent axes: source (who grants it), bucket (how it is HELD), and recovery (how it is CAST).

source.description: Key into sources. Supplies the ability/DC/attack used to cast this spell.

bucket.description: How the spell is HELD. cantrip = chosen cantrip (counts against cantrips_known). prepared = leveled spell prepared from budget (counts against prepared_limit). always = additive grant (subclass domain/oath spells, feat grants — does NOT count against budget). known = in spellbook but not currently prepared. class_list = added to class spell list as learnable option (subclass expanded-spell-list grants, NOT auto-prepared).

recovery.description: How the spell is CAST. at_will = cantrip. spell_slot = via leveled slot pool. pact_slot = via Pact Magic slots. slotless_per_rest = once/short-or-long with its own use pool. ritual_only = castable only as a ritual.

ritual_castable.description: Can be cast as a ritual (+10 min, no slot) even when bucket='known' and not currently prepared.

save_ability.description: Ability catalog identifier for the saving throw this spell forces, or null.

attack_kind.description: Attack type catalog identifier (melee_spell, ranged_spell), or null.

material_cost_gp.description: Cost of consumed or focus material in gp. null = no cost.

material_consumed.description: Whether the material component is consumed by the casting.

casting_time.description: Structured casting time. null = not yet populated from DB.

value.description: For minute/hour: how many. null for action/bonus/reaction.

range.description: Structured range. null = not yet populated from DB.

distance.description: For distance: range in feet. null for non-distance kinds.

duration.description: Structured duration. null = not yet populated from DB.

value.description: For round/minute/hour/day: how many. null for instantaneous/until_dispelled/special.

concentration.description: Whether this duration requires concentration to maintain.

uses.description: For slotless_per_rest spells: the max uses pool. Absent for at-will and slot-cast spells.

secondary_cast.description: An additional casting avenue for the same (name, source). E.g. a granted spell castable slotless once per rest that is ALSO slot-castable. The limited avenue is primary recovery; the fallback goes here.

pool.description: A maximum count (no remaining). Used for spell uses budgets and secondary cast pools.

recharge.description: Recharge trigger identifier(s). Single trigger, or a list when more than one applies (e.g. short OR long rest).
